description: This classic Hot and Sour Soup is a beloved staple of Chinese cuisine, renowned for its exhilarating balance of spicy and tangy flavors. Each spoonful offers a delightful medley of textures from tender pork, tofu, and mushrooms, all swimming in a rich, savory broth. It is the perfect comforting dish to awaken your palate and warm your soul.

ingredients.notes: mixed with 4 tbsp cold water for slurry

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rehydrate dried mushrooms in warm water for 20 minutes, then drain, squeeze dry, and julienne. Thinly julienne the pork tenderloin, tofu, and bamboo shoots. Prepare the cornstarch slurry by mixing cornstarch with cold water.

instructions.title: Simmer Broth and Solids
instructions.text: In a large pot, bring chicken broth to a boil. Add the julienned pork and cook until it changes color, about 1-2 minutes. Then add the rehydrated mushrooms, tofu, and bamboo shoots. Reduce heat to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Season the Soup Base
instructions.text: Stir in the soy sauce, black vinegar, and white pepper. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed, adding more vinegar or pepper for desired hot and sour balance. The soup should have a distinct tangy and spicy kick.

instructions.title: Thicken the Soup
instructions.text: Stir the cornstarch slurry well before slowly pouring it into the simmering soup in a thin stream, stirring constantly. Continue to stir until the soup thickens to your desired consistency. This usually takes 1-2 minutes.

instructions.title: Add Eggs and Finish
instructions.text: Once the soup is thickened, slowly drizzle the beaten eggs into the simmering soup in a thin stream while gently stirring in one direction. This creates delicate egg ribbons. Turn off the heat immediately after adding the eggs.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Stir in the sesame oil just before serving. Ladle the Hot and Sour Soup into individual bowls. Garnish generously with sliced green onions and a drizzle of chili oil if desired for extra heat. Serve immediately and enjoy the authentic flavors.

tips: For the best texture, ensure your cornstarch slurry is well mixed before adding to prevent lumps, and stir continuously while pouring it in.
Authentic Chinese black vinegar is key to the soup's distinctive tang. Do not substitute with regular white vinegar if you want the true flavor profile.
Hot and Sour Soup is best enjoyed fresh. If you have leftovers, store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2-3 days and reheat gently on the stovetop.
article:
Dive into the delightful world of Chinese cuisine, and you’ll quickly discover a soup that tantalizes the taste buds with its complex symphony of flavors: Hot and Sour Soup. More than just a starter, this iconic dish is a culinary journey, renowned for its intriguing balance of piquant spice and tangy zest, all wrapped in a comforting, savory broth. It's a staple in Chinese restaurants worldwide and a beloved comfort food for countless enthusiasts. But what exactly makes this soup so captivating, and how can you recreate its magic in your own kitchen? Let's unravel the secrets behind this classic.

## What is Hot and Sour Soup?

Hot and Sour Soup, or Suan La Tang in Mandarin, is a quintessential Chinese soup characterized by its distinctive dual flavor profile: a fiery kick from chili and white pepper, and a refreshing tang from vinegar. Beyond these dominant tastes, it boasts a savory depth, often enriched with ingredients like soy sauce and sesame oil, and a subtle sweetness that rounds out the experience. The texture is equally important – traditionally thickened with cornstarch, it has a luscious, velvety consistency that coats the palate beautifully.

Originating from Szechuan cuisine, known for its bold and spicy flavors, Hot and Sour Soup has evolved over centuries. While regional variations exist, the core elements remain consistent: a hearty broth brimming with an array of finely shredded ingredients. Common additions include bamboo shoots, wood ear mushrooms (or dried shiitake mushrooms), tofu, and often shredded pork or chicken, though vegetarian versions are also incredibly popular. Each spoonful offers a delightful interplay of textures, from the tender protein to the crisp vegetables, all swimming in that unforgettable broth. It’s a soup that warms you from the inside out, making it particularly popular during colder months or as a refreshing appetizer any time of year.

## Ingredients You Need for Hot and Sour Soup

Creating an authentic Hot and Sour Soup recipe at home requires a thoughtful selection of ingredients, many of which are readily available in most supermarkets or Asian grocery stores. Don't be intimidated by the list; each component plays a crucial role in building the soup's signature flavor and texture.

For the Broth Base:
*   6 cups chicken broth (or vegetable broth for a vegetarian version)
*   1 tablespoon soy sauce (light or regular)
*   1 tablespoon dark soy sauce (for color and richer flavor, optional)
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar (or black vinegar for a deeper tang)
*   1-2 tablespoons chili oil (adjust to your spice preference)
*   1 teaspoon white pepper (crucial for that authentic "hot" kick)
*   1/2 teaspoon sesame oil

For the Fillings:
*   4 ounces shredded pork loin or chicken breast (or extra firm tofu, pressed and diced, for vegetarian)
*   4 ounces firm or silken tofu, cut into small matchsticks
*   1/2 cup canned bamboo shoots, julienned
*   1/2 cup dried wood ear mushrooms (or dried shiitake mushrooms), rehydrated and thinly sliced
*   1 large egg, whisked

For Thickening and Garnish:
*   2 tablespoons cornstarch mixed with 4 tablespoons water (cornstarch slurry)
*   2 tablespoons chopped green onions
*   Optional: a dash more rice vinegar or chili oil for serving

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Hot and Sour Soup

Mastering this classic Hot and Sour Soup recipe is simpler than you might think. Follow these steps for a truly delicious bowl:

1.  **Prepare Ingredients:** If using dried mushrooms, rehydrate them in warm water for at least 20-30 minutes, then drain, squeeze dry, and slice thinly. Shred your chosen protein (pork or chicken). Cut tofu and bamboo shoots into small, uniform matchsticks. Whisk the egg in a small bowl. Prepare the cornstarch slurry.
2.  **Simmer the Broth:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, bring the chicken or vegetable broth to a gentle simmer over medium heat.
3.  **Add Protein and Vegetables:** Add the shredded pork/chicken (or diced firm tofu for vegetarian) to the simmering broth. Cook for 2-3 minutes until the protein is just cooked through. Then, add the sliced wood ear mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and matchstick tofu. Continue to simmer for another 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
4.  **Season the Soup:** Stir in the soy sauce, dark soy sauce (if using), rice vinegar, chili oil, and white pepper. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed. This is where you fine-tune the "hot" and "sour" balance. You might want a little more vinegar for tang or more white pepper for heat.
5.  **Thicken the Soup:** Give the cornstarch slurry a quick stir (it separates quickly). Slowly pour the slurry into the simmering soup in a thin, steady stream, stirring continuously. Continue to stir until the soup thickens to your desired consistency. It should be noticeably thicker but still pourable.
6.  **Add Egg Swirls:** With the soup still simmering gently, slowly drizzle the whisked egg into the soup in a circular motion. Let it cook undisturbed for about 30 seconds to form delicate ribbons, then gently stir once or twice to distribute the egg.
7.  **Finish and Serve:** Remove the soup from the heat. Stir in the sesame oil. Ladle the Hot and Sour Soup into bowls, garnish with chopped green onions, and serve immediately.

## Pro Tips for the Best Hot and Sour Soup Results

Achieving that perfect restaurant-quality Hot and Sour Soup recipe at home involves a few key techniques and considerations:

*   **Balance is Key:** The most crucial aspect of Hot and Sour Soup is the balance between the hot (white pepper, chili oil) and sour (vinegar) elements. Don't be afraid to taste and adjust these during the seasoning step. Add vinegar incrementally until you hit your preferred tang. Similarly, add white pepper and chili oil gradually to control the heat.
*   **Quality Ingredients:** While many ingredients are simple, using good quality broth, fresh tofu, and authentic Chinese vinegars (like Chinkiang black vinegar) can elevate the flavor significantly.
*   **Don't Skimp on White Pepper:** White pepper is distinct from black pepper. It provides a unique, pungent heat that is essential for the authentic "hot" component of this soup.
*   **Cornstarch Slurry Technique:** Ensure your cornstarch slurry is well-mixed before adding it, and pour it slowly while stirring continuously to prevent lumps and achieve a smooth, silky texture. If the soup isn't thick enough, you can always mix a little more cornstarch with water and add it gradually.
*   **Egg Ribbons:** For delicate egg ribbons, ensure your soup is at a gentle simmer, not a rolling boil. Drizzle the egg slowly from a height and let it set for a moment before stirring gently.
*   **Freshness Matters:** Garnish with fresh green onions just before serving for a vibrant pop of color and a fresh aromatic finish.

## Serving Suggestions for Hot and Sour Soup

Hot and Sour Soup is incredibly versatile and pairs beautifully with a variety of Chinese dishes, or even stands wonderfully on its own.

*   **As an Appetizer:** It's a classic starter for any Chinese meal. Its bold flavors awaken the palate, preparing it for the dishes to come.
*   **With Rice or Noodles:** A bowl of Hot and Sour Soup, alongside a simple bowl of steamed white rice, makes for a light yet satisfying meal. You can also serve it with stir-fried noodles or lo mein.
*   **Alongside Main Courses:** It complements rich and savory main courses such as General Tso's Chicken, Kung Pao Shrimp, or Beef with Broccoli. The soup's acidity and spice cut through richer flavors, offering a refreshing contrast.
*   **Dim Sum Companion:** Enjoy it as part of a dim sum spread, providing a warm and flavorful counterpoint to steamed buns and dumplings.
*   **Comfort Food Meal:** On a chilly evening, a large bowl of this soup can be a complete, comforting meal in itself, especially if you add extra protein and vegetables.

## Variations and Substitutions for Hot and Sour Soup

One of the great things about this Hot and Sour Soup recipe is its adaptability. Feel free to experiment and tailor it to your dietary preferences or what you have on hand.

*   **Protein Alternatives:** Instead of pork or chicken, consider using thinly sliced beef, shrimp, or even fish. For a vegetarian or vegan version, increase the amount of tofu (both firm and silken work well) or add rehydrated textured vegetable protein (TVP).
*   **Vegetable Additions:** Napa cabbage, carrots, snow peas, water chestnuts, or even baby corn can be excellent additions, providing extra texture and nutrients. Just ensure they are thinly sliced to cook quickly and blend well.
*   **Spice Level:** If you prefer less heat, reduce the amount of white pepper and chili oil. For an extra fiery kick, add fresh finely minced bird’s eye chilies or a dash of Sriracha.
*   **Sourness:** While rice vinegar is standard, Chinkiang black vinegar offers a deeper, more complex tang. Lemon juice or lime juice can also be used in a pinch for acidity, though it will alter the authentic flavor profile slightly.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Ensure you use gluten-free soy sauce (tamari) and verify that your broth and chili oil are also gluten-free.
*   **Keto/Low-Carb:** This soup is already quite low-carb. Just ensure you use a sugar-free broth and omit any ingredients that might contain hidden sugars. The cornstarch thickener can be reduced slightly or replaced with a small amount of xanthan gum for a keto-friendly version.

The journey to making your own Hot and Sour Soup is a rewarding one. With its incredible depth of flavor and comforting warmth, it’s a dish that truly satisfies. So gather your ingredients, follow this Hot and Sour Soup recipe, and prepare to impress your taste buds with a homemade classic that rivals any restaurant version. Enjoy the perfect blend of hot, sour, and savory in every delicious spoonful!
